content:
We are trying to start the initial setup for Feastfite, a webapp that has a theme of players are little food monsters and 

Context
Monster grubby guy icons, food-themed territorial webapp built from a blank repo. Users claim restaurant territories by uploading meal photos, compete via timed votes, and hold/defend land for points. The goal of this plan is to define the microservices architecture, tech stack, and how to split work cleanly across 4 developers so nobody is blocked on day 1.

Tech Stack
Layer
Choice
Rationale
Backend
Node.js + TypeScript (Express)
Consistent language across all 5 services; any dev can help any other
Frontend
React + TypeScript (Vite)
Fast DX, strong typing, component-per-developer ownership
Map
Leaflet.js + React-Leaflet
Open-source, no API key friction, polygon/overlay support
Real-time
Socket.io (vote-service only)
WebSocket rooms keyed by voting session ID
API Gateway
Kong 3.6 (declarative, no DB)
JWT validation, rate-limiting, CORS, WebSocket proxy — zero custom code
Primary DB
PostgreSQL 16 (one per service)
ACID, relational, familiar
Geospatial
PostGIS on territory-service Postgres
Polygon boundary and lat/lng queries
Cache / timers
Redis 7
Lock TTL keys, leaderboard sorted sets, token blocklist
Event bus
RabbitMQ 3.13 (topic exchange)
Async fan-out between services; simpler ops than Kafka at this scale
Object storage
MinIO (S3-compatible, self-hosted)
Food photos + avatars; swap for S3 in production
Container
Docker + Kubernetes
Each service is its own container + K8s Deployment


Microservices
auth-service
Register, login, JWT (15min access + 7d refresh httpOnly cookie)
Refresh token rotation with Redis blocklist
Google OAuth2 (stretch)
territory-service
Territory polygon CRUD with PostGIS
Claim state machine: unclaimed → voting → claimed → locked
12-hour lock stored as lockedUntil timestamp (Postgres authoritative) + Redis TTL key for events
Battering-ram unlock: calls economy-service to deduct item, then clears lock
Territory shield: blocks ownership change on vote.winner_declared
Consumes: vote.winner_declared → updates owner, color, name, dish photo
Emits: territory.claimed
vote-service
MinIO presigned URL generation for photo upload (frontend uploads directly to MinIO)
10-minute voting session with Socket.io room per session
Vote tally + winner selection; tie-breaking by random if equal
Emits: vote.winner_declared, vote.participant
economy-service
Append-only points ledger (user_id, delta, reason, created_at)
Shop catalog (seeded): double-points 100pts, shield 200pts, battering-ram 500pts
Item inventory per user
IAP webhook (Stripe test mode → award points)
Consumes: vote.winner_declared, vote.participant, territory.claimed
profile-service
Profile CRUD (avatar via MinIO avatars bucket)
Achievements engine (JSON config, event-driven evaluator)
Streak cron (node-cron inside container, daily check)
Redis leaderboard sorted sets (ZADD leaderboard:global)
Clan CRUD: create, join, leave, clan leaderboard
Consumes: territory.claimed, vote.winner_declared, item.purchased, user.registered

Inter-Service Communication
Synchronous REST (via API Gateway):
Frontend → all services
territory-service → economy-service (synchronous inventory check for battering-ram)
Async RabbitMQ (topic exchange foodwars.events):
vote-service       --[vote.winner_declared]--> territory-service, economy-service, profile-service
vote-service       --[vote.participant]-------> economy-service
territory-service  --[territory.claimed]------> profile-service, economy-service
economy-service    --[item.purchased]----------> territory-service, profile-service
auth-service       --[user.registered]---------> profile-service
WebSocket (internal to vote-service only):
wss://api/ws/vote/{sessionId} proxied by Kong
Used only for live vote count updates during 10-minute windows
All other timers (lock countdown) are frontend-computed from lockedUntil timestamp

4-Developer Delegation
Dev A — "Gatekeeper" (Auth + Infrastructure)
Owns: auth-service, Kong gateway config, base docker-compose, K8s namespace/configmap/secrets-template, k8s/gateway/, k8s/auth/
Backend deliverables:
JWT issuance, refresh rotation, /auth/register, /auth/login, /auth/refresh, /auth/logout
Redis refresh token blocklist
Emits user.registered
Frontend deliverables:
src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx — JWT storage in memory + httpOnly refresh cookie
PrivateRoute wrapper (used by all other devs to gate pages)
LoginPage, RegisterPage, ForgotPasswordPage
Unblocks everyone: AuthContext + PrivateRoute should land by end of day 1.

Dev B — "Mapmaker" (Territory Service)
Owns: territory-service, PostGIS setup, k8s/territory/
Backend deliverables:
GET /territories?bbox=minLng,minLat,maxLng,maxLat (polygon bounding box)
GET /territories/:id
POST /territories/:id/claim (starts vote session via vote-service or directly if uncontested)
POST /territories/:id/battering-ram (checks/deducts from economy-service, clears lock)
RabbitMQ consumer: vote.winner_declared → commit ownership
Territory polygon seed script (~50 territories around a test city)
Frontend deliverables:
MapView (Leaflet + React-Leaflet, colored polygons per owner)
TerritoryPanel (sidebar: owner, dish photo, avatar, lock countdown)
ClaimButton (triggers upload flow owned by Dev C)
LockCountdown (pure frontend timer from lockedUntil)

Dev C — "Referee" (Vote + Upload Service)
Owns: vote-service, MinIO setup, RabbitMQ StatefulSet, k8s/vote/, k8s/infra/minio.yml, k8s/infra/rabbitmq.yml
Backend deliverables:
POST /votes/upload-url → returns MinIO presigned PUT URL
POST /votes/sessions → creates voting session { territoryId, photoKey }
Socket.io server: rooms per session, broadcast live vote counts
10-minute window timer (Redis distributed lock to prevent duplicate on restart)
Tally + emit vote.winner_declared, vote.participant
Frontend deliverables:
UploadModal — camera/file picker, photo preview, submit
VotingRoom — live vote counts, candidate dishes, vote button, countdown (WebSocket-driven)
WinnerAnnouncement — overlay shown to all session participants

Dev D — "Economist" (Economy + Profile Services)
Owns: economy-service, profile-service, Redis StatefulSet, k8s/economy/, k8s/profile/, k8s/infra/redis.yml
Backend deliverables (economy):
Append-only points ledger
GET /economy/balance/:userId, POST /economy/award, POST /economy/spend
Shop item catalog + POST /economy/shop/purchase
Item inventory CRUD
Stripe IAP webhook stub
Backend deliverables (profile):
Profile CRUD + avatar upload (MinIO avatars bucket)
Achievement definitions (JSON seed) + event-driven evaluator
Daily streak cron (node-cron)
Redis sorted-set leaderboard
Clan CRUD
Frontend deliverables:
ProfilePage (stats, achievements, streak, cosmetic decor picker)
ShopPage (item catalog, points balance, purchase button)
InventoryWidget (header: active buffs/items)
LeaderboardPage (global + clan tabs)
ClanPage (create/join, member list)

Monorepo Structure
team_j-bert/
├── docker-compose.yml              ← Dev A writes; all infra services + service stubs
├── .env.example                    ← All env vars documented
├── events/schemas/                 ← Shared RabbitMQ event type definitions (agree day 1)
│   ├── vote.winner_declared.ts
│   ├── territory.claimed.ts
│   ├── item.purchased.ts
│   └── user.registered.ts
├── packages/shared/                ← Internal npm package @foodwars/shared
│   ├── types/                      ← Shared TypeScript interfaces
│   └── utils/
│       ├── jwt.ts                  ← JWT verify helper (all services use this)
│       └── amqp.ts                 ← RabbitMQ connection factory
├── services/
│   ├── api-gateway/                ← kong.yml + Dockerfile
│   ├── auth-service/               ← Dev A
│   ├── territory-service/          ← Dev B
│   ├── vote-service/               ← Dev C
│   ├── economy-service/            ← Dev D
│   └── profile-service/            ← Dev D
├── frontend/                       ← React/Vite; components divided by dev
│   └── src/
│       ├── contexts/AuthContext.tsx ← Dev A (unblocks all others)
│       ├── components/
│       │   ├── map/                ← Dev B
│       │   ├── voting/             ← Dev C
│       │   ├── economy/            ← Dev D
│       │   └── profile/            ← Dev D
│       └── api/                    ← Typed API clients per service
└── k8s/
    ├── namespace.yml               ← Dev A
    ├── infra/                      ← postgres, redis (D), rabbitmq (C), minio (C)
    ├── gateway/                    ← Dev A
    ├── auth/                       ← Dev A
    ├── territory/                  ← Dev B
    ├── vote/                       ← Dev C
    ├── economy/                    ← Dev D
    └── profile/                    ← Dev D

Image Upload Flow (no binary traffic through Kong)
Frontend: POST /api/v1/votes/upload-url → vote-service returns MinIO presigned PUT URL
Frontend uploads image directly to MinIO (bypass gateway)
Frontend: POST /api/v1/votes/sessions with { territoryId, photoKey }

Critical Path
Day 1 (team alignment — 30 min meeting)
Agree and commit:
events/schemas/*.ts type definitions
JWT payload shape: { sub: userId, email, iat, exp }
API URL prefix: /api/v1/{service}/{resource}
docker-compose.yml with all infra running
Week 1 — Parallel scaffolding (all 4 devs simultaneously)
A: Auth skeleton + AuthContext + PrivateRoute + docker-compose
B: Territory PostGIS schema + GET /territories?bbox + Leaflet map with seeded data
C: MinIO setup + photo upload + Socket.io hello-world
D: Points ledger endpoints + shop catalog + profile CRUD
Week 2 — Core gameplay loop
Goal: see map → upload photo → vote → territory changes color
A: Full auth flows working
B: Claim submission + RabbitMQ consumer for vote.winner_declared → color change on map
C: 10-min voting window + Socket.io voting room + emit vote.winner_declared
D: RabbitMQ consumer awarding points, balance visible in header
Week 3 — Economy and social
B: Battering-ram unlock, territory shield
C: Tie-breaking, edge cases, photo moderation stub
D: Shop purchase flow, IAP webhook, clan CRUD, achievements, streak cron
Week 4 — K8s and polish
All devs write K8s manifests for their services; Dev A wires CI/CD (GitHub Actions build + push images).

Verification
docker-compose up brings all 5 services + infra online locally
Smoke test: register → login → see map → upload food photo → vote → territory claims
K8s: kubectl apply -f k8s/ deploys to local kind/minikube cluster
Each service has a GET /health endpoint returning { status: "ok", service: "..." }
RabbitMQ management UI (localhost:15672) confirms events are flowing between services

Critical Files to Create First
events/schemas/vote.winner_declared.ts — shared contract; all 3 downstream devs depend on it
packages/shared/utils/amqp.ts — shared RabbitMQ factory prevents divergent connection code
docker-compose.yml — every dev needs Postgres, Redis, RabbitMQ, MinIO running locally
services/auth-service/src/index.ts — JWT middleware; needed by all services
frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx — needed by all frontend components



the plan is to implement it all in 1 week. we want the initial setup before the 4 devs start

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture. The repo is essentially empty. I need to create a plan for the initial setup that needs to happen before the 4 developers start working.

Based on the detailed architecture description, the "initial setup" should include:

1. **Monorepo scaffolding** - Directory structure
2. **Shared infrastructure** - docker-compose.yml with all infra services
3. **Event schemas** - The shared type definitions all devs need
4. **Shared packages** - @foodwars/shared with JWT utilities and AMQP factory
5. **Service stubs** - Basic Express+TypeScript skeletons for all 5 services with /health endpoints


6. **Frontend scaffold** - Vite+React+TypeScript with AuthContext stub
7. **Kong API Gateway config** - kong.yml
8. **K8s namespace and base configs**
9. **.env.example** - All environment variables documented
10. **.gitignore**

But thinking about what's actually critical for the 4 devs to start working, I should focus on the essential contracts and utilities: the shared event type definitions, the RabbitMQ factory, and JWT verification utilities. Here is the complete, file-by-file implementation plan.

---

# FeastFite Monorepo — Implementation Plan

## Environment Baseline

- Node.js: v24.12.0, npm: 11.7.0
- Docker: 29.2.0
- No pnpm or yarn present — use **npm workspaces** throughout
- Key package versions pinned below are the latest stable as of this plan

---

## Part 1 — Decision Record (Read This Before Writing a Single File)

### 1.1 npm workspaces vs pnpm

Use npm workspaces. pnpm is not installed, and npm 11 workspaces are mature. The root `package.json` declares `"workspaces": ["packages/*", "services/*", "frontend"]`. This means `npm install` at the root hoists all deps into the root `node_modules` with symlinks for workspace packages. One gotcha: `@feastfite/shared` will be resolvable from any service package only after it has been `npm install`-ed at the root level; do not run `npm install` in individual service directories until the root install has been done at least once.

### 1.2 TypeScript Module Resolution

Use `"moduleResolution": "bundler"` in `tsconfig.base.json` for the frontend (Vite uses bundler resolution). Use `"moduleResolution": "node16"` for all Node.js services to get proper ESM/CJS interop. Services will compile to CommonJS (`"module": "commonjs"`) so they work without `.js` extension imports. Set `"paths"` in `tsconfig.base.json` to map `@feastfite/shared` to `../../packages/shared/src` — this is for editor IntelliSense only; the runtime resolution uses the npm workspace symlink.

### 1.3 Dockerfile Strategy

All service Dockerfiles use a two-stage build:
- Stage 1 (`builder`): `node:24-alpine`, copies the entire monorepo root (needed for workspace symlinks), runs `npm ci`, then `npm run build -w services/<name>`.
- Stage 2 (`runtime`): `node:24-alpine`, copies only `dist/` and `node_modules/` from the builder stage. This avoids shipping TypeScript source.

Critical gotcha: When building a service image from a monorepo, the Docker build context must be the **repo root**, not the service subdirectory. The `docker-compose.yml` must set `context: .` and `dockerfile: services/<name>/Dockerfile`. Every service Dockerfile must therefore start with `WORKDIR /app` and `COPY . .`.

### 1.4 Kong Declarative Format

Kong 3.6 uses `_format_version: "3.0"` in `kong.yml`. Do not use `"2.1"` — it is deprecated in 3.x. The Kong Gateway OSS image `kong:3.6` is used. The environment variable `KONG_DATABASE=off` and `KONG_DECLARATIVE_CONFIG=/etc/kong/kong.yml` activate DB-less mode.

### 1.5 PostGIS Image

Use `postgis/postgis:16-3.4` (the official image combining PostgreSQL 16 + PostGIS 3.4). This image automatically loads the `postgis` extension but you still need `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS postgis;` in your migration scripts.

### 1.6 RabbitMQ Management UI

Use `rabbitmq:3.13-management` (not the plain `rabbitmq:3.13`). The `-management` tag bundles the HTTP management plugin on port 15672.

### 1.7 `.env` Strategy

A single `.env.example` at the repo root documents all variables. Each service also has its own `.env` at `services/<name>/.env` for local `npm run dev` runs outside Docker. These per-service `.env` files are gitignored. The `docker-compose.yml` uses `env_file` directives pointing to these files, with environment variable overrides for Docker networking (e.g., `DB_HOST=postgres-auth` instead of `localhost`).

### 1.8 Event Schema Location

`events/schemas/` contains pure TypeScript interface files — no runtime code. They are not a separate npm package; they are copied (or path-aliased) into each service that needs them. The simpler approach: add `events/` to the root `tsconfig.base.json` `include` path and reference it from `@feastfite/shared`'s re-exports.

---

## Part 2 — Exact File Creation Order

The order matters because of dependency chains:

```
Phase A — Root scaffolding (no deps between files)
  1.  .gitignore
  2.  .env.example
  3.  tsconfig.base.json
  4.  package.json  (root, declares workspaces)

Phase B — Shared package (services depend on this)
  5.  packages/shared/package.json
  6.  packages/shared/tsconfig.json
  7.  packages/shared/types/index.ts
  8.  packages/shared/utils/jwt.ts
  9.  packages/shared/utils/amqp.ts

Phase C — Event schemas (used by shared + services)
  10. events/schemas/vote.winner_declared.ts
  11. events/schemas/territory.claimed.ts
  12. events/schemas/item.purchased.ts
  13. events/schemas/user.registered.ts

Phase D — Service stubs (all 5, can be done in parallel by 4 devs)
  For each of: auth, territory, vote, economy, profile:
  14. services/<name>/package.json
  15. services/<name>/tsconfig.json
  16. services/<name>/src/index.ts
  17. services/<name>/Dockerfile
  18. services/<name>/.env

Phase E — API Gateway
  19. services/api-gateway/kong.yml
  20. services/api-gateway/Dockerfile

Phase F — Frontend
  21. frontend/package.json
  22. frontend/tsconfig.json + tsconfig.node.json
  23. frontend/vite.config.ts
  24. frontend/index.html
  25. frontend/src/main.tsx
  26. frontend/src/App.tsx
  27. frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx
  28. frontend/src/components/PrivateRoute.tsx
  29. frontend/src/api/authApi.ts
  30. frontend/src/api/territoryApi.ts
  31. frontend/src/api/voteApi.ts
  32. frontend/src/api/economyApi.ts
  33. frontend/src/api/profileApi.ts
  34. frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx
  35. frontend/src/pages/RegisterPage.tsx

Phase G — Infrastructure
  36. docker-compose.yml
  37. k8s/namespace.yml
  38. k8s/infra/postgres.yml
  39. k8s/infra/redis.yml
  40. k8s/infra/rabbitmq.yml
  41. k8s/infra/minio.yml
```

---

## Part 3 — File-by-File Content Specification

### Phase A — Root Scaffolding

---

**File 1: `.gitignore`**

```
# Dependencies
node_modules/
.npm/

# Build output
dist/
build/

# Environment files (per-service .env, never commit real secrets)
services/**/.env
frontend/.env
.env
!.env.example

# TypeScript incremental build info
*.tsbuildinfo

# Editor
.vscode/
.idea/
*.sw[po]

# OS
.DS_Store
Thumbs.db

# Docker local volumes
.docker-volumes/

# Logs
*.log
npm-debug.log*
```

Key decision: `.env.example` is committed (it has placeholder values). All real `.env` files are gitignored. The `!.env.example` negation pattern is required because the `services/**/.env` glob would otherwise catch `.env.example` if it were in a service directory.

---

**File 2: `.env.example`**

This file is documentation. Every variable used anywhere in the project lives here with a safe default or a `CHANGE_ME` marker.

```dotenv
# ── Shared JWT ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
JWT_SECRET=CHANGE_ME_use_openssl_rand_base64_32
JWT_EXPIRES_IN=15m

# ── PostgreSQL ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
POSTGRES_USER=feastfite
POSTGRES_PASSWORD=CHANGE_ME

AUTH_DB_HOST=localhost
AUTH_DB_PORT=5432
AUTH_DB_NAME=auth_db

TERRITORY_DB_HOST=localhost
TERRITORY_DB_PORT=5433
TERRITORY_DB_NAME=territory_db

VOTE_DB_HOST=localhost
VOTE_DB_PORT=5434
VOTE_DB_NAME=vote_db

ECONOMY_DB_HOST=localhost
ECONOMY_DB_PORT=5435
ECONOMY_DB_NAME=economy_db

PROFILE_DB_HOST=localhost
PROFILE_DB_PORT=5436
PROFILE_DB_NAME=profile_db

# ── Redis ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379

# ── RabbitMQ ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
RABBITMQ_URL=amqp://guest:guest@localhost:5672
RABBITMQ_EXCHANGE=feastfite.events

# ── MinIO ────────────────────────────────────────────────�

The territory Postgres overrides the `image:` so it cannot use the anchor directly. 4. These are intentionally minimal — in real Kubernetes you would use Helm charts (bitnami/postgresql, bitnami/redis, etc.). --- ## Part 4 — Developer Parallelism Strategy Once Phase A and Phase B are complete (root + shared package), 4 developers can work in parallel: | Dev | Work Package | |-----|-------------| | Dev 1 | `services/auth-service` — full implementation (JWT issuance, pg schema, Redis sessions) | | Dev 2 | `services/territory-service` — PostGIS queries, territory claim logic, event publishing | | Dev 3 | `services/vote-service` + `services/economy-service` — vote session state machine, points ledger | | Dev 4 | `frontend` — React components, routing, API client integration | Profile-service can be picked up by any dev who finishes early, as it has no cross-service dependencies at the stub phase. **Conflict zones to communicate about:** - `packages/shared/src/types/index.ts` — all devs will want to add fields. Establish that type additions are append-only (no breaking changes to existing interfaces) and require a short Slack thread before merging. - `docker-compose.yml` — only infra changes; agree that service additions are the only permitted changes during parallel dev phase. - `services/api-gateway/kong.yml` — route additions only; no changes to existing plugin config. --- ## Part 5 — Verification Checklist ### Step 1: Local typecheck (no Docker needed) ```bash # From repo root, after creating all files: npm install npm run build -w packages/shared npm run typecheck # should report 0 errors ``` ### Step 2: Individual service health checks ```bash # Terminal 1 (start infra only first): docker-compose up postgres-auth redis rabbitmq -d # Terminal 2: npm run dev -w services/auth-service # Expected: "[auth-service] listening on port 3001" curl http://localhost:3001/health # Expected: {"status":"ok","service":"auth-service"} ``` ### Step 3: Full Docker Compose stack ```bash docker-compose up --build ``` Wait for all healthchecks to pass (roughly 60 seconds on first run due to PostGIS initialization). Then: ```bash # Health check all services via Kong curl http://localhost:8000/api/auth/health # → {"status":"ok","service":"auth-service"} curl http://localhost:8000/api/territory/health curl http://localhost:8000/api/vote/health curl http://localhost:8000/api/economy/health curl http://localhost:8000/api/profile/health # Kong admin (list configured routes) curl http://localhost:8001/routes | jq '.data[].name' # RabbitMQ management UI open http://localhost:15672 # guest/guest # MinIO console open http://localhost:9001 # minioadmin/minioadmin123 ``` ### Step 4: Frontend dev server ```bash npm run dev -w frontend # Opens http://localhost:5173 # /login and /register routes should render # / should redirect to /login ``` ### Step 5: Validate PostGIS ```bash docker exec -it feastfite-postgres-territory psql -U feastfite -d territory_db \ -c "SELECT PostGIS_Version();" # Expected: "3.4 ..." — confirms PostGIS is active ``` --- ## Part 6 — Common Gotchas Reference | Gotcha | Problem | Fix | |--------|---------|-----| | `@feastfite/shared` not found at runtime | `dist/` not built before Docker build | Always run `npm run build -w packages/shared` in CI before building service images | | `ECONNREFUSED` on RabbitMQ at service startup | Race condition: service starts before RabbitMQ is ready | The `amqp.ts` reconnect loop handles this — just set `depends_on: rabbitmq: condition: service_healthy` | | Kong returns 401 on `/health` | Global JWT plugin enabled | Disable JWT plugin globally; apply it only to authenticated routes via `route:` scoping in `kong.yml` | | PostGIS `CREATE EXTENSION` fails | Extension not pre-loaded | `postgis/postgis:16-3.4` auto-creates it, but only for the default DB. Run `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS postgis;` in migration scripts for `territory_db` | | Windows bind mount permission errors | Docker Desktop + Windows NTFS | Use named volumes (already done in `docker-compose.yml`) instead of `./data:/var/lib/postgresql/data` bind mounts | | npm workspace install installs wrong version | Running `npm install` in a service subdirectory | Always run `npm install` from the repo root. Per-subdirectory installs bypass hoisting | | Vite HMR breaks behind Kong proxy | WebSocket HMR traffic not proxied | Vite dev server runs on 5173 directly; only API calls proxy through Kong. Do not proxy `localhost:5173` through Kong | | `tsx watch` fails on Windows | Path separators in globs | Use `tsx watch src/index.ts` (explicit path, not glob) — already specified in all service `dev` scripts | | Kong `_format_version: "2.1"` warning | Deprecated format in Kong 3.x | Use `"3.0"` — already specified in the plan | | `docker-compose up` hangs on PostGIS | `postgis/postgis:16-3.4` initializes extensions on first start, slow | The healthcheck uses `pg_isready` which returns true before extension init completes. Add a 5-second `start_period` to the PostGIS healthcheck | --- ## Part 7 — Files NOT Created (Intentional Omissions) These are deferred to after the parallel dev phase: - **Migration scripts** (`services//migrations/`) — schema not finalized - **Unit/integration tests** — no test runner configured yet (Vitest recommended) - **CI pipeline** (`.github/workflows/`) — deferred until services compile cleanly - **Kubernetes service/deployment YAMLs** — only namespace + infra placeholders now - **MinIO bucket initialization script** — use a one-time job or the console at first - **ESLint/Prettier config** — add after parallel dev phase to avoid merge conflicts on formatting --- ### Critical Files for Implementation - `/c/Users/creat/Desktop/learning/scope/FeastFite/package.json` - `/c/Users/creat/Desktop/learning/scope/FeastFite/tsconfig.base.json` - `/c/Users/creat/Desktop/learning/scope/FeastFite/packages/shared/src/utils/amqp.ts` - `/c/Users/creat/Desktop/learning/scope/FeastFite/docker-compose.yml` - `/c/Users/creat/Desktop/learning/scope/FeastFite/services/api-gateway/kong.yml`

# FeastFite — Initial Monorepo Setup Plan

## Context

The FeastFite repo is a blank slate (just README.md + git). Before 4 developers start parallel feature work, we need the full monorepo skeleton in place: shared contracts, service stubs, infra config, and the frontend scaffold. Without this, Dev A, B, C, D are blocked waiting on each other's config. This plan delivers everything needed for Day 1 parallel work.

## Key Decisions (locked in)

| Decision | Choice | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Package manager | npm workspaces | npm 11 is already installed; no pnpm |
| Service runtime | tsx watch | Faster than ts-node, no ESM loader fiddling |
| Express version | Express 5.x | Built-in async error handling |
| TS moduleResolution | `node16` for services, `bundler` for frontend | Vite requires bundler; Node16 for CJS services |
| Kong config format | `_format_version: "3.0"` | 2.1 is deprecated in Kong 3.x |
| Docker volumes | Named volumes (not bind mounts) | Prevents NTFS permission errors on Windows |
| JWT storage (frontend) | In-memory React state only | Prevents XSS token theft from localStorage |
| Event schemas | Re-exported from `@feastfite/shared` | Avoids standalone non-npm path-alias complexity |
| Kong JWT plugin | Disabled globally in initial setup | DB-less mode needs consumers defined first |

## File Creation Order

Dependencies chain bottom-up. Create in this order:

### Phase A — Root Scaffolding
1. `.gitignore`
2. `.env.example` — all env vars documented with `CHANGE_ME` markers
3. `tsconfig.base.json` — `node16` module resolution, CommonJS output, paths for `@feastfite/shared`
4. `package.json` (root) — npm workspaces: `["packages/*", "services/auth-service", ..., "frontend"]`

### Phase B — Shared Package (all services depend on this)
5. `packages/shared/package.json` — name: `@feastfite/shared`, deps: amqplib, jsonwebtoken
6. `packages/shared/tsconfig.json` — extends base, `composite: true`, clears `paths`
7. `packages/shared/src/types/index.ts` — User, Territory, VoteSession, PointsLedgerEntry, Achievement, Clan interfaces + FeastFiteEvent union
8. `packages/shared/src/utils/jwt.ts` — `verifyToken()` + `signToken()` using jsonwebtoken
9. `packages/shared/src/utils/amqp.ts` — `createAmqpConnection()` factory with reconnect loop (up to 10 attempts, 5s delay, `process.exit(1)` on permanent failure)
10. `packages/shared/src/index.ts` — barrel re-exports (with `.js` extensions for node16)

### Phase C — Event Schemas (inside shared package)
11. `events/schemas/vote.winner_declared.ts` — `{ eventType, sessionId, territoryId, winnerId, winnerPhotoKey, timestamp }`
12. `events/schemas/territory.claimed.ts` — `{ eventType, territoryId, newOwnerId, previousOwnerId, timestamp }`
13. `events/schemas/item.purchased.ts` — `{ eventType, userId, itemId, itemType, pointsSpent, timestamp }`
14. `events/schemas/user.registered.ts` — `{ eventType, userId, email, timestamp }`
   - Re-export all 4 from `packages/shared/src/types/index.ts`

### Phase D — Service Stubs (5 services, same pattern)
For each of: auth (3001), territory (3002), vote (3003), economy (3004), profile (3005):
- `services/<name>/package.json` — deps: express, cors, helmet, pg, ioredis, amqplib, dotenv, `@feastfite/shared: "*"`
- `services/<name>/tsconfig.json` — extends base, `composite: true`, references `packages/shared`
- `services/<name>/src/index.ts` — Express app, `GET /health → { status: "ok", service: "<name>" }`
- `services/<name>/Dockerfile` — 2-stage: builder copies full monorepo, `npm ci --workspace`, builds shared then service; runtime copies only dist + node_modules
- `services/<name>/.env` — local dev env vars (gitignored)

### Phase E — API Gateway
- `services/api-gateway/kong.yml` — `_format_version: "3.0"`, services/routes for all 5 services + WS route for vote-service, CORS plugin, rate-limiting (120/min), JWT plugin **disabled** until consumers set up
- `services/api-gateway/Dockerfile` — `FROM kong:3.6`, copies kong.yml

### Phase F — Frontend
- `frontend/package.json` — deps: react 19, react-dom, react-router-dom 7, axios, `@feastfite/shared: "*"`
- `frontend/tsconfig.json` — `moduleResolution: bundler`, `jsx: react-jsx`, paths for `@feastfite/shared` (one level up: `../packages/shared/src/index.ts`)
- `frontend/tsconfig.node.json` — for vite.config.ts
- `frontend/vite.config.ts` — React plugin, alias for `@feastfite/shared`, proxy `/api` → `http://localhost:8000` (Kong), proxy `/ws` → `ws://localhost:8000`
- `frontend/index.html`
- `frontend/src/main.tsx`
- `frontend/src/App.tsx` — BrowserRouter + AuthProvider, routes: /login, /register, / (PrivateRoute)
- `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx` — user + token in React state (in-memory), login/logout/register functions
- `frontend/src/components/PrivateRoute.tsx` — redirects to /login via `<Navigate>` if not authenticated
- `frontend/src/api/authApi.ts` — axios client, baseURL `/api/auth`, login/register/me
- `frontend/src/api/territoryApi.ts`, `voteApi.ts`, `economyApi.ts`, `profileApi.ts` — stub axios clients

### Phase G — Kubernetes Base
- `k8s/namespace.yml` — namespace: feastfite
- `k8s/infra/postgres.yml` — StatefulSet + headless Service per DB (5 total)
- `k8s/infra/redis.yml`, `rabbitmq.yml`, `minio.yml` — StatefulSet + headless Service stubs

### Phase H — docker-compose.yml (ties everything together)
- 5 Postgres instances (ports 5432–5436); territory uses `postgis/postgis:16-3.4`
- Redis 7 (6379)
- RabbitMQ 3.13-management (5672 + 15672)
- MinIO latest (9000 + 9001)
- 5 service containers: `build: { context: ., dockerfile: services/<name>/Dockerfile }`, environment overrides for Docker network hostnames
- Kong 3.6: `KONG_DATABASE=off`, `KONG_DECLARATIVE_CONFIG=/etc/kong/kong.yml`, mounts `kong.yml` as read-only volume
- All services use `depends_on: condition: service_healthy` to prevent race conditions on first start

## Critical Files

| File | Owner | Blocks |
|---|---|---|
| `packages/shared/src/utils/amqp.ts` | Initial setup | All services (RabbitMQ consumers) |
| `packages/shared/src/utils/jwt.ts` | Initial setup | All services (auth middleware) |
| `events/schemas/*.ts` | Initial setup | territory-service, economy-service, profile-service |
| `docker-compose.yml` | Initial setup | All devs (local infra) |
| `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx` | Initial setup / Dev A | All frontend components |
| `services/auth-service/src/index.ts` | Initial setup / Dev A | Kong JWT setup |

## Important Gotchas

1. **`@feastfite/shared` dist must be built first** — run `npm run build -w packages/shared` before any service or before `docker-compose up --build`. Document in README.
2. **Dockerfiles use `context: .` (repo root)** — never set the build context to a service subdirectory; workspace symlinks won't resolve.
3. **node16 TS requires `.js` extensions in imports** — barrel re-exports in shared must use `export * from './utils/jwt.js'` (not `.ts`); TypeScript resolves `.js` → `.ts` at compile time.
4. **PostGIS slow init** — add `start_period: 30s` to PostGIS healthcheck to avoid false-negative health failures.
5. **Kong JWT plugin** — start disabled globally, scope to specific routes once auth-service issues real JWTs and consumers are added to kong.yml.
6. **npm installs always from root** — never run `npm install` inside a service directory until root install has been done; workspace hoisting won't work otherwise.

## Verification

```bash
# 1. TypeCheck (no Docker needed)
npm install
npm run build -w packages/shared
npm run typecheck   # expect 0 errors

# 2. Infra smoke test
docker-compose up postgres-auth redis rabbitmq -d
npm run dev -w services/auth-service
curl http://localhost:3001/health  # → {"status":"ok","service":"auth-service"}

# 3. Full stack
docker-compose up --build
# Wait ~60s for PostGIS init

curl http://localhost:8000/api/auth/health       # via Kong
curl http://localhost:8000/api/territory/health
curl http://localhost:8000/api/vote/health
curl http://localhost:8000/api/economy/health
curl http://localhost:8000/api/profile/health

# 4. Verify PostGIS
docker exec feastfite-postgres-territory psql -U feastfite -d territory_db -c "SELECT PostGIS_Version();"

# 5. RabbitMQ UI: http://localhost:15672 (guest/guest)
# 6. MinIO console: http://localhost:9001 (minioadmin/minioadmin123)
# 7. Frontend: npm run dev -w frontend → http://localhost:5173 (redirects to /login)
```

- `frontend/package.json` — deps: react 19, react-dom, react-router-dom 7, axios, `@feastfite/shared: "*"`

- `frontend/src/App.tsx` — BrowserRouter + AuthProvider, routes: /login, /register, / (PrivateRoute)
- `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx` — user + token in React state (in-memory), login/logout/register functions
- `frontend/src/components/PrivateRoute.tsx` — redirects to /login via `<Navigate>` if not authenticated
- `frontend/src/api/authApi.ts` — axios client, baseURL `/api/auth`, login/register/me
- `frontend/src/api/territoryApi.ts`, `voteApi.ts`, `economyApi.ts`, `profileApi.ts` — stub axios clients

- `k8s/namespace.yml` — namespace: feastfite
- `k8s/infra/postgres.yml` — StatefulSet + headless Service per DB (5 total)
- `k8s/infra/redis.yml`, `rabbitmq.yml`, `minio.yml` — StatefulSet + headless Service stubs

- MinIO latest (9000 + 9001)

# FeastFite — Initial Monorepo Setup Plan

## Context

The FeastFite repo is a blank slate (just README.md + git). Before 4 developers start parallel feature work, we need the full monorepo skeleton in place: shared contracts, service stubs, infra config, and the frontend scaffold. Without this, Dev A, B, C, D are blocked waiting on each other's config. This plan delivers everything needed for Day 1 parallel work.

## Key Decisions (locked in)

| Decision | Choice | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Package manager | npm workspaces | npm 11 is already installed; no pnpm |
| Service runtime | tsx watch | Faster than ts-node, no ESM loader fiddling |
| Express version | Express 5.x | Built-in async error handling |
| TS moduleResolution | `node16` for services, `bundler` for frontend | Vite requires bundler; Node16 for CJS services |
| Kong config format | `_format_version: "3.0"` | 2.1 is deprecated in Kong 3.x |
| Docker volumes | Named volumes (not bind mounts) | Prevents NTFS permission errors on Windows |
| JWT storage (frontend) | In-memory React state only | Prevents XSS token theft from localStorage |
| Event schemas | Re-exported from `@feastfite/shared` | Avoids standalone non-npm path-alias complexity |
| Kong JWT plugin | Disabled globally in initial setup | DB-less mode needs consumers defined first |

## File Creation Order

Dependencies chain bottom-up. Create in this order:

### Phase A — Root Scaffolding
1. `.gitignore`
2. `.env.example` — all env vars documented with `CHANGE_ME` markers

6. `packages/shared/tsconfig.json` — extends base, `composite: true`, clears `paths`
7. `packages/shared/src/types/index.ts` — User, Territory, VoteSession, PointsLedgerEntry, Achievement, Clan interfaces + FeastFiteEvent union
8. `packages/shared/src/utils/jwt.ts` — `verifyToken()` + `signToken()` using jsonwebtoken
9. `packages/shared/src/utils/amqp.ts` — `createAmqpConnection()` factory with reconnect loop (up to 10 attempts, 5s delay, `process.exit(1)` on permanent failure)
10. `packages/shared/src/index.ts` — barrel re-exports (with `.js` extensions for node16)

### Phase C — Event Schemas (inside shared package)
11. `events/schemas/vote.winner_declared.ts` — `{ eventType, sessionId, territoryId, winnerId, winnerPhotoKey, timestamp }`
12. `events/schemas/territory.claimed.ts` — `{ eventType, territoryId, newOwnerId, previousOwnerId, timestamp }`
13. `events/schemas/item.purchased.ts` — `{ eventType, userId, itemId, itemType, pointsSpent, timestamp }`
14. `events/schemas/user.registered.ts` — `{ eventType, userId, email, timestamp }`
   - Re-export all 4 from `packages/shared/src/types/index.ts`

### Phase D — Service Stubs (5 services, same pattern)
For each of: auth (3001), territory (3002), vote (3003), economy (3004), profile (3005):
- `services/<name>/package.json` — deps: express, cors, helmet, pg, ioredis, amqplib, dotenv, `@feastfite/shared: "*"`
- `services/<name>/tsconfig.json` — extends base, `composite: true`, references `packages/shared`
- `services/<name>/src/index.ts` — Express app, `GET /health → { status: "ok", service: "<name>" }`
- `services/<name>/Dockerfile` — 2-stage: builder copies full monorepo, `npm ci --workspace`, builds shared then service; runtime copies only dist + node_modules
- `services/<name>/.env` — local dev env vars (gitignored)

### Phase E — API Gateway
- `services/api-gateway/kong.yml` — `_format_version: "3.0"`, services/routes for all 5 services + WS route for vote-service, CORS plugin, rate-limiting (120/min), JWT plugin **disabled** until consumers set up
- `services/api-gateway/Dockerfile` — `FROM kong:3.6`, copies kong.yml

### Phase F — Frontend
- `frontend/package.json` — deps: react 19, react-dom, react-router-dom 7, axios, `@feastfite/shared: "*"`
- `frontend/tsconfig.json` — `moduleResolution: bundler`, `jsx: react-jsx`, paths for `@feastfite/shared` (one level up: `../packages/shared/src/index.ts`)
- `frontend/tsconfig.node.json` — for vite.config.ts
- `frontend/vite.config.ts` — React plugin, alias for `@feastfite/shared`, proxy `/api` → `http://localhost:8000` (Kong), proxy `/ws` → `ws://localhost:8000`
- `frontend/index.html`
- `frontend/src/main.tsx`
- `frontend/src/App.tsx` — BrowserRouter + AuthProvider, routes: /login, /register, / (PrivateRoute)
- `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx` — user + token in React state (in-memory), login/logout/register functions
- `frontend/src/components/PrivateRoute.tsx` — redirects to /login via `<Navigate>` if not authenticated
- `frontend/src/api/authApi.ts` — axios client, baseURL `/api/auth`, login/register/me
- `frontend/src/api/territoryApi.ts`, `voteApi.ts`, `economyApi.ts`, `profileApi.ts` — stub axios clients

### Phase G — Kubernetes Base
- `k8s/namespace.yml` — namespace: feastfite
- `k8s/infra/postgres.yml` — StatefulSet + headless Service per DB (5 total)
- `k8s/infra/redis.yml`, `rabbitmq.yml`, `minio.yml` — StatefulSet + headless Service stubs

### Phase H — docker-compose.yml (ties everything together)
- 5 Postgres instances (ports 5432–5436); territory uses `postgis/postgis:16-3.4`
- Redis 7 (6379)
- RabbitMQ 3.13-management (5672 + 15672)
- MinIO latest (9000 + 9001)
- 5 service containers: `build: { context: ., dockerfile: services/<name>/Dockerfile }`, environment overrides for Docker network hostnames
- Kong 3.6: `KONG_DATABASE=off`, `KONG_DECLARATIVE_CONFIG=/etc/kong/kong.yml`, mounts `kong.yml` as read-only volume
- All services use `depends_on: condition: service_healthy` to prevent race conditions on first start

## Critical Files

| File | Owner | Blocks |
|---|---|---|
| `packages/shared/src/utils/amqp.ts` | Initial setup | All services (RabbitMQ consumers) |
| `packages/shared/src/utils/jwt.ts` | Initial setup | All services (auth middleware) |
| `events/schemas/*.ts` | Initial setup | territory-service, economy-service, profile-service |
| `docker-compose.yml` | Initial setup | All devs (local infra) |
| `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx` | Initial setup / Dev A | All frontend components |
| `services/auth-service/src/index.ts` | Initial setup / Dev A | Kong JWT setup |

## Important Gotchas

1. **`@feastfite/shared` dist must be built first** — run `npm run build -w packages/shared` before any service or before `docker-compose up --build`. Document in README.
2. **Dockerfiles use `context: .` (repo root)** — never set the build context to a service subdirectory; workspace symlinks won't resolve.
3. **node16 TS requires `.js` extensions in imports** — barrel re-exports in shared must use `export * from './utils/jwt.js'` (not `.ts`); TypeScript resolves `.js` → `.ts` at compile time.
4. **PostGIS slow init** — add `start_period: 30s` to PostGIS healthcheck to avoid false-negative health failures.
5. **Kong JWT plugin** — start disabled globally, scope to specific routes once auth-service issues real JWTs and consumers are added to kong.yml.
6. **npm installs always from root** — never run `npm install` inside a service directory until root install has been done; workspace hoisting won't work otherwise.

## Verification

```bash
# 1. TypeCheck (no Docker needed)
npm install
npm run build -w packages/shared
npm run typecheck   # expect 0 errors

# 2. Infra smoke test
docker-compose up postgres-auth redis rabbitmq -d
npm run dev -w services/auth-service
curl http://localhost:3001/health  # → {"status":"ok","service":"auth-service"}

# 3. Full stack
docker-compose up --build
# Wait ~60s for PostGIS init

curl http://localhost:8000/api/auth/health       # via Kong
curl http://localhost:8000/api/territory/health
curl http://localhost:8000/api/vote/health
curl http://localhost:8000/api/economy/health
curl http://localhost:8000/api/profile/health

# 4. Verify PostGIS
docker exec feastfite-postgres-territory psql -U feastfite -d territory_db -c "SELECT PostGIS_Version();"

# 5. RabbitMQ UI: http://localhost:15672 (guest/guest)
# 6. MinIO console: http://localhost:9001 (minioadmin/minioadmin123)
# 7. Frontend: npm run dev -w frontend → http://localhost:5173 (redirects to /login)
```

# ── Stage 1: Builder ─────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app

COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
COPY packages/shared/package.json ./packages/shared/
COPY services/auth-service/package.json ./services/auth-service/
COPY tsconfig.base.json ./
COPY packages/shared ./packages/shared
COPY services/auth-service ./services/auth-service

RUN npm ci --workspace=packages/shared --workspace=services/auth-service

RUN npm run build -w packages/shared
RUN npm run build -w services/auth-service

# ── Stage 2: Runtime ─────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM node:20-alpine AS runtime
WORKDIR /app

ENV NODE_ENV=production

COPY --from=builder /app/packages/shared/dist ./packages/shared/dist
COPY --from=builder /app/packages/shared/package.json ./packages/shared/package.json
COPY --from=builder /app/services/auth-service/dist ./services/auth-service/dist
COPY --from=builder /app/services/auth-service/package.json ./services/auth-service/package.json
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./package.json

WORKDIR /app/services/auth-service
EXPOSE 3001

CMD ["node", "dist/index.js"]

_format_version: "3.0"

services:
  - name: auth-service
    url: http://auth-service:3001
    routes:
      - name: auth-routes
        paths:
          - /api/auth
        strip_path: false
        protocols: [http, https]

  - name: territory-service
    url: http://territory-service:3002
    routes:
      - name: territory-routes
        paths:
          - /api/territory
        strip_path: false
        protocols: [http, https]

  - name: vote-service
    url: http://vote-service:3003
    routes:
      - name: vote-routes
        paths:
          - /api/vote
        strip_path: false
        protocols: [http, https]
      - name: vote-ws-route
        paths:
          - /ws/vote
        strip_path: false
        protocols: [ws, wss]

  - name: economy-service
    url: http://economy-service:3004
    routes:
      - name: economy-routes
        paths:
          - /api/economy
        strip_path: false
        protocols: [http, https]

  - name: profile-service
    url: http://profile-service:3005
    routes:
      - name: profile-routes
        paths:
          - /api/profile
        strip_path: false
        protocols: [http, https]

plugins:
  # JWT plugin — disabled in initial setup.
  # Enable and scope to specific routes once auth-service issues JWTs
  # and consumers with JWT credentials are added to this file.
  - name: jwt
    enabled: false
    config:
      secret_is_base64: false
      claims_to_verify:
        - exp

  - name: rate-limiting
    config:
      minute: 120
      hour: 3000
      policy: local
      fault_tolerant: true
      hide_client_headers: false

  - name: cors
    config:
      origins:
        - "http://localhost:5173"
        - "https://feastfite.com"
      methods:
        - GET
        - POST
        - PUT
        - PATCH
        - DELETE
        - OPTIONS
      headers:
        - Accept
        - Authorization
        - Content-Type
      credentials: true
      max_age: 3600

# ── auth-db ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: postgres-auth
  namespace: feastfite
spec:
  serviceName: postgres-auth
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: postgres-auth
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: postgres-auth
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: auth_db
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: postgres-credentials
                  key: username
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: postgres-credentials
                  key: password
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:

version: "3.9"

x-postgres-common: &postgres-common
  image: postgres:16-alpine
  restart: unless-stopped
  environment:
    POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-feastfite}
    POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-devpassword}
  healthcheck:
    test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-feastfite}"]
    interval: 10s
    timeout: 5s
    retries: 5
    start_period: 10s

services:
  # ── Databases ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  postgres-auth:
    <<: *postgres-common
    container_name: feastfite-postgres-auth
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-feastfite}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-devpassword}
      POSTGRES_DB: auth_db
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres-auth-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data

  postgres-territory:
    container_name: feastfite-postgres-territory
    image: postgis/postgis:16-3.4
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-feastfite}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-devpassword}
      POSTGRES_DB: territory_db
    ports:
      - "5433:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres-territory-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-feastfite}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 30s

  postgres-vote:
    <<: *postgres-common
    container_name: feastfite-postgres-vote
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-feastfite}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-devpassword}
      POSTGRES_DB: vote_db
    ports:
      - "5434:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres-vote-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data

  postgres-economy:
    <<: *postgres-common
    container_name: feastfite-postgres-economy
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-feastfite}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-devpassword}
      POSTGRES_DB: economy_db
    ports:
      - "5435:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres-economy-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data

  postgres-profile:
    <<: *postgres-common
    container_name: feastfite-postgres-profile
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-feastfite}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-devpassword}
      POSTGRES_DB: profile_db
    ports:
      - "5436:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres-profile-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data

  # ── Redis ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: feastfite-redis
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
    volumes:
      - redis-data:/data
    command: redis-server --appendonly yes
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  # ── RabbitMQ ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  rabbitmq:
    image: rabbitmq:3.13-management
    container_name: feastfite-rabbitmq
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "5672:5672"
      - "15672:15672"
    environment:
      RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER: ${RABBITMQ_USER:-guest}
      RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS: ${RABBITMQ_PASS:-guest}
    volumes:
      - rabbitmq-data:/var/lib/rabbitmq
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "rabbitmq-diagnostics", "-q", "ping"]
      interval: 15s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 20s

  # ── MinIO ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  minio:
    image: minio/minio:latest
    container_name: feastfite-minio
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: server /data --console-address ":9001"
    ports:
      - "9000:9000"
      - "9001:9001"
    environment:
      MINIO_ROOT_USER: ${MINIO_ACCESS_KEY:-minioadmin}
      MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${MINIO_SECRET_KEY:-minioadmin123}
    volumes:
      - minio-data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mc", "ready", "local"]
      interval: 15s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s

  # ── Application Services ──────────────────────────────────────────
  auth-service:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: services/auth-service/Dockerfile
    container_name: feastfite-auth-service
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "3001:3001"
    environment:
      PORT: "3001"
      NODE_ENV: development
      DB_HOST: postgres-auth
      DB_PORT: "5432"
      DB_NAME: auth_db
      DB_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-feastfite}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-devpassword}
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      RABBITMQ_URL: amqp://${RABBITMQ_USER:-guest}:${RABBITMQ_PASS:-guest}@rabbitmq:5672
      RABBITMQ_EXCHANGE: feastfite.events
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:-dev_secret_CHANGE_IN_PROD}
      JWT_EXPIRES_IN: ${JWT_EXPIRES_IN:-15m}
    depends_on:
      postgres-auth:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
      rabbitmq:
        condition: service_healthy

  territory-service:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: services/territory-service/Dockerfile
    container_name: feastfite-territory-service
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "3002:3002"
    environment:
      PORT: "3002"
      NODE_ENV: development
      DB_HOST: postgres-territory
      DB_PORT: "5432"
      DB_NAME: territory_db
      DB_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-feastfite}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-devpassword}
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      RABBITMQ_URL: amqp://${RABBITMQ_USER:-guest}:${RABBITMQ_PASS:-guest}@rabbitmq:5672
      RABBITMQ_EXCHANGE: feastfite.events
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:-dev_secret_CHANGE_IN_PROD}
    depends_on:
      postgres-territory:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
      rabbitmq:
        condition: service_healthy

  vote-service:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: services/vote-service/Dockerfile
    container_name: feastfite-vote-service
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "3003:3003"
    environment:
      PORT: "3003"
      NODE_ENV: development
      DB_HOST: postgres-vote
      DB_PORT: "5432"
      DB_NAME: vote_db
      DB_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-feastfite}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-devpassword}
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      RABBITMQ_URL: amqp://${RABBITMQ_USER:-guest}:${RABBITMQ_PASS:-guest}@rabbitmq:5672
      RABBITMQ_EXCHANGE: feastfite.events
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:-dev_secret_CHANGE_IN_PROD}
      MINIO_ENDPOINT: minio
      MINIO_PORT: "9000"
      MINIO_ACCESS_KEY: ${MINIO_ACCESS_KEY:-minioadmin}
      MINIO_SECRET_KEY: ${MINIO_SECRET_KEY:-minioadmin123}
    depends_on:
      postgres-vote:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
      rabbitmq:
        condition: service_healthy

  economy-service:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: services/economy-service/Dockerfile
    container_name: feastfite-economy-service
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "3004:3004"
    environment:
      PORT: "3004"
      NODE_ENV: development
      DB_HOST: postgres-economy
      DB_PORT: "5432"
      DB_NAME: economy_db
      DB_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-feastfite}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-devpassword}
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      RABBITMQ_URL: amqp://${RABBITMQ_USER:-guest}:${RABBITMQ_PASS:-guest}@rabbitmq:5672
      RABBITMQ_EXCHANGE: feastfite.events
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:-dev_secret_CHANGE_IN_PROD}
    depends_on:
      postgres-economy:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
      rabbitmq:
        condition: service_healthy

  profile-service:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: services/profile-service/Dockerfile
    container_name: feastfite-profile-service
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "3005:3005"
    environment:
      PORT: "3005"
      NODE_ENV: development
      DB_HOST: postgres-profile
      DB_PORT: "5432"
      DB_NAME: profile_db
      DB_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-feastfite}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-devpassword}
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      RABBITMQ_URL: amqp://${RABBITMQ_USER:-guest}:${RABBITMQ_PASS:-guest}@rabbitmq:5672
      RABBITMQ_EXCHANGE: feastfite.events
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:-dev_secret_CHANGE_IN_PROD}
    depends_on:
      postgres-profile:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
      rabbitmq:
        condition: service_healthy

  # ── Kong API Gateway ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  kong:
    image: kong:3.6
    container_name: feastfite-kong
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      KONG_DATABASE: "off"
      KONG_DECLARATIVE_CONFIG: /etc/kong/kong.yml
      KONG_PROXY_ACCESS_LOG: /dev/stdout
      KONG_ADMIN_ACCESS_LOG: /dev/stdout
      KONG_PROXY_ERROR_LOG: /dev/stderr
      KONG_ADMIN_ERROR_LOG: /dev/stderr
      KONG_ADMIN_LISTEN: "0.0.0.0:8001"
      KONG_PROXY_LISTEN: "0.0.0.0:8000"
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
      - "8001:8001"
    volumes:
      - ./services/api-gateway/kong.yml:/etc/kong/kong.yml:ro
    depends_on:
      - auth-service
      - territory-service
      - vote-service
      - economy-service
      - profile-service

volumes:
  postgres-auth-data:
  postgres-territory-data:
  postgres-vote-data:
  postgres-economy-data:
  postgres-profile-data:
  redis-data:
  rabbitmq-data:
  minio-data:

Initial monorepo scaffold is complete and all TypeScript compiles clean.

**Why:** Pre-work before 4 developers start parallel feature development. All shared contracts and service stubs must exist so no dev is blocked on day 1.

## Key decisions locked in

- JWT stored in React memory only (not localStorage) — XSS prevention

- `npx tsc -p services/<name>/tsconfig.json --noEmit` — typecheck a service

## amqplib note
`amqplib@0.10.x` `connect()` returns `ChannelModel` not `Connection`. The shared `amqp.ts` uses `ChannelModel` accordingly.

All done. Here's a summary of what was scaffolded — 60+ files, zero TypeScript errors:

---

**Initial setup complete.** All 4 devs can start immediately.

## What was built

| Phase | Files |
|---|---|
| Root | `.gitignore`, `.env.example`, `tsconfig.base.json`, `package.json` (npm workspaces) |
| `@feastfite/shared` | Types (User, Territory, VoteSession, etc.), `verifyToken`/`signToken`, `createAmqpConnection` with reconnect loop, all 4 RabbitMQ event interfaces |
| Event schemas | `events/schemas/` — re-exports from shared (single source of truth) |
| 5 service stubs | `auth/territory/vote/economy/profile-service` — each has `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `src/index.ts` with `/health` endpoint, `Dockerfile` (2-stage), `.env` |
| API gateway | `kong.yml` (format 3.0) — all routes, CORS + rate-limiting, JWT plugin disabled until consumers are set up |
| Frontend | Vite + React 18 + TypeScript — `AuthContext`, `PrivateRoute`, 5 typed API clients, `LoginPage`, `RegisterPage` |
| K8s | `namespace.yml` + `infra/` StatefulSets for all 5 Postgres, Redis, RabbitMQ, MinIO |
| `docker-compose.yml` | All infra + 5 services + Kong; PostGIS gets `start_period: 30s`; all services use `condition: service_healthy` |

## One gotcha fixed

TS 5.8 rejects `module: commonjs` + `moduleResolution: node16` together — switched to `moduleResolution: node` (stable CJS mode, no `.js` extension requirements).

## Day 1 command for devs

```bash
# Clone, install, build shared, spin up infra
npm install
npm run build -w packages/shared
docker-compose up postgres-auth redis rabbitmq -d

# Then start your service
npm run dev -w services/auth-service   # Dev A
npm run dev -w services/territory-service   # Dev B
# etc.
```

input.prompt: The user is asking how to share a Claude Code conversation with others. Does Claude Code have a feature to share or export conversations? What are the options available?

content.text:
## Claude Code Conversation Sharing & Export Options

Claude Code doesn't have a built-in feature to generate shareable links, but here are your available options:

### Built-in Export Commands

**`/export`** - Opens a dialog to copy or save your conversation to a file
- Type `/export` alone to choose how to export
- Use `/export filename.md` to save directly to disk with a specific name
- Example: `/export conversation-log.md`

### Manual Sharing Methods

1. **Export as Markdown** - Use `/export` to save conversations as readable text files you can email or share
2. **Remote Control** - Use `/remote-control` to let someone control your session directly from claude.ai
3. **Direct File Access** - Conversations are stored as `.jsonl` files in `~/.claude/projects/<project-name>/` — you can copy and share these raw files directly

### Naming & Organization

- **`/rename`** - Rename sessions for easier tracking: `/rename auth-refactor`
- **Launch with name** - Start a new session with a name: `claude -n "payment-webhook-debug"`

### Community Tools

If you need more advanced sharing, community-developed tools are available:

- **[claude-code-share](https://github.com/wsxiaoys/claude-code-share)** - Generate shareable links from conversations
- **[claude-conversation-extractor](https://github.com/ZeroSumQuant/claude-conversation-extractor)** - Extract conversations from Claude Code's internal storage
- **[claude-chat-exporter](https://github.com/agarwalvishal/claude-chat-exporter)** - Export conversations to formatted Markdown

---

**Sources:**
- [How to export, save, and share a Claude Code conversation](https://kentgigger.com/posts/claude-code-export-save-conversation)
- [Sharing and Unsharing Chats | Claude Help Center](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/10593882-sharing-and-unsharing-chats)
- [claude-code-share - GitHub](https://github.com/wsxiaoys/claude-code-share)
- [claude-conversation-extractor - GitHub](https://github.com/ZeroSumQuant/claude-conversation-extractor)
